# SessionTranscript golden vectors

SYNTHETIC, generated by TestGenerateSessionTranscriptGoldens from the
OID4VPHandover / OID4VPDCAPIHandover constructors (sessiontranscript.go) with
the fixed inputs in sessiontranscript_test.go (tClientID, tNonce, tThumbprint,
tRespURI, tOrigin).

Encoding (revised 2026-07-06, docs/mdoc-eu-gap-report.md — see that report for
full detail):
  SessionTranscript          = [null, null, Handover]
  OID4VPHandover              = ["OpenID4VPHandover", SHA-256(CBOR(OpenID4VPHandoverInfo))]
  OpenID4VPHandoverInfo       = [client_id, nonce, jwkThumbprint, response_uri]
  OID4VPDCAPIHandover         = ["OpenID4VPDCAPIHandover", SHA-256(CBOR(OpenID4VPDCAPIHandoverInfo))]
  OpenID4VPDCAPIHandoverInfo  = [origin, nonce, jwkThumbprint]
  jwkThumbprint = RFC 7638 thumbprint of the RP's ephemeral encryption key
    (present only for an encrypted/direct_post.jwt response), else CBOR null.

This replaces an earlier, PENDING-flagged 3-hash-tuple construction that was
never cross-checked against any real implementation. Corrected 2026-07-06
against `references/eudi-srv-verifier-endpoint-main` (the EU reference RP
verifier, `DocumentValidator.kt`/`HandoverInfo.kt`) after the OpenID4VP 1.0
Annex B text could not be fetched in that session (WebFetch truncates before
reaching Annex B on the full spec HTML; no vendored copy under references/).
The outer shape and inner element order/membership are well-corroborated
against that production reference; `jwkThumbprint`'s exact CBOR type (tstr
assumed) is NOT yet confirmed against primary spec text.

STILL PENDING: vendor the OpenID4VP 1.0 spec text (or ISO/IEC TS 18013-7 Annex
B) under references/ and do a final byte-for-byte confirmation, mirroring how
`references/statuslist-draft12.txt`/`-draft21.txt` closed the equivalent gap
for go-statuslist — before wiring an encrypted-response (WP-08) deployment
against these constructors.
